pick.rationale: Despite the significant loss of star guard Aden Holloway due to a felony drug arrest, Alabama maintains a substantial talent advantage with elite offensive efficiency (rank 3) and superior KenPom/Torvik rankings (16/17) compared to Hofstra (87/92). The Crimson Tide's SEC-tested depth and overall athleticism should overwhelm the Pride, though Holloway's absence narrows the margin in this 4/13 matchup. Hofstra lacks the quality wins and schedule strength to capitalize fully on Alabama's off-court turmoil.

reasoningStep.title: Alabama vs Hofstra Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama is the clear favorite despite losing Aden Holloway to arrest; Hofstra lacks the metrics to pull off a major upset.
evidence: Alabama ranks 16/17 in KenPom/Torvik with elite offense (rank 3) vs Hofstra's 87/92 rankings.
Aden Holloway's arrest and suspension significantly weaken Alabama's backcourt, but the Crimson Tide still have superior depth and athleticism.
Hofstra's CAA schedule (24-10 record) lacks quality wins against top-50 opponents, making a 13-over-4 upset unlikely despite Alabama's turmoil.
Historical 4/13 upsets occur about 20% of the time, but Alabama's talent disparity and tournament experience should prevail.
